title: Share of people who feel close to at least one person
caption:
Share of people who feel close to at least one person

Share of respondents who said "yes" when asked whether there is a special person they feel very close to.

This horizontal bar chart presents 2023 data on the share of people who feel close to at least one person across 25 countries and territories. The bars are arranged in descending order of percentage, ranging from a high of 93% to a low of 67%. Nigeria leads the dataset with 93% of respondents affirming they have a special person they feel very close to, followed closely by the Philippines at 90%. A cluster of nations including Israel, Egypt, Spain, Sweden, and the United States occupy the upper tier, with values between 86% and 88%. The middle section of the chart shows a tight grouping where Poland, Germany, Hong Kong, Australia, South Africa, and the United Kingdom all report shares between 82% and 85%. Further down the list, Kenya, India, Brazil, Argentina, and Mexico display values ranging from 79% to 82%. Indonesia and Turkey both register at 77%, while Tanzania sits slightly lower at 75%. Japan appears at the bottom of the ranking with 67%, representing the lowest share among the displayed locations. The visual distribution indicates that in every country shown, a majority of the population reports feeling close to at least one person, with the gap between the highest and lowest recorded shares spanning 26 percentage points.
